priority_actions.summary: Maintain the explicit engineering mirror contract and keep the mirror lane synchronized.

priority_actions.summary: Maintain the curated basename-divergence registry instead of re-triaging the same files.
priority_actions.why: 5 manual-review collisions are now classified in the divergence registry; unresolved_private_shadow=1.

priority_actions.summary: Maintain the dedicated private-memory shadow contract and keep nested memory lanes out of public cleanup.
priority_actions.why: 1 deferred private-shadow basename collision is now governed by a dedicated shadow contract; current pair_count=2.

priority_actions.summary: Backfill Purpose and Last Updated metadata on public entrypoint documents.
priority_actions.why: 3 root entrypoints are still missing Purpose and/or date hints, which makes retrieval agents infer scope from prose instead of metadata.

priority_actions.summary: Maintain the historical document lane contract so archive and chronicle surfaces stay governed without per-file manual metadata backfill.
priority_actions.why: 49 historical-lane docs are now governed by a dedicated policy and report; current chronicle_markdown_count=19.

priority_actions.summary: Maintain the paradox casebook-to-fixture contract instead of treating all fixture drift as duplication.
priority_actions.why: 8 paradox fixture basename collisions are now governed by an ownership map; current needs_review_count=0.

entries.title: ToneSoul A/B/C Firewall Doctrine
entries.purpose: document governance, claim boundaries, and theory-to-mechanism separation

entries.title: ToneSoul Adaptive Deliberation Mode Contract
entries.purpose: define when ToneSoul deliberation should stay lightweight, use standard council, or elevate to full multi-round council — mapped against task track, readiness, risk, and claim state

entries.title: ToneSoul Adversarial Deliberation Adoption Map
entries.purpose: organize council improvement families into adoption lanes based on current ToneSoul infrastructure, classify each by readiness and risk, and give later agents a bounded shortlist instead of a speculative roadmap

entries.title: ToneSoul Audience Routing And Entry Contract
entries.purpose: define the cleanest first-hop reading path for each audience so later readers stop mistaking historical, deep, or operational surfaces for the first thing to open.

entries.title: ToneSoul Axiom Falsification Map
entries.purpose: make ToneSoul axioms challengeable by explicit support and weakening conditions without mutating AXIOMS.json prematurely

entries.title: ToneSoul Claim Authority Matrix
entries.purpose: tell later agents, for each major ToneSoul claim or term, what its current authority and implementation status is

entries.title: ToneSoul Claim-to-Evidence Matrix
entries.purpose: map high-value ToneSoul claims to their actual evidence level, strongest backing source, weakest link, and safest phrasing for later agents

entries.title: ToneSoul Claude-Compatible Entry Adapter Contract
entries.purpose: define one repo-native adapter that lets Claude-style shells cold-start from ToneSoul without drifting away from the same first-hop order used by Codex and dashboard/operator shells.

entries.title: ToneSoul Context Continuity Adoption Map
entries.purpose: translate high-level context-transfer ideas into ToneSoul-native continuity lanes so later agents can preserve structure across sessions, tasks, agents, and models without turning handoff memory into hidden magic.
entries.status: active adoption map and implementation-order aid.

entries.title: ToneSoul Continuity Import And Decay Contract
entries.purpose: define what a receiving agent may import directly, what remains advisory, what decays, and what requires explicit re-confirmation — per continuity surface

entries.title: ToneSoul Continuity Surface Lifecycle Map
entries.purpose: organize continuity surfaces into lifecycle lanes with explicit lifetime, refresh triggers, decay triggers, and receiver behavior — so later agents and Codex can manage continuity as a system rather than a flat list

entries.title: ToneSoul Council Confidence And Calibration Map
entries.purpose: map every confidence-bearing surface in the council system, classify each as descriptive or calibrated, name the evidence gap between current state and calibrated state, and identify which calibration approaches are plausible now versus infrastructure-blocked

entries.title: ToneSoul Council Dossier And Dissent Contract
entries.purpose: define the minimum shape of a ToneSoul decision dossier, which dissent must survive the verdict, what counts as confidence posture, and what later agents may safely replay versus what remains opaque

entries.title: ToneSoul Council Realism And Independence Contract
entries.purpose: define which parts of the current council are truly independent, which are perspective-voiced but not independent, and which claims later agents must avoid making about council realism

entries.title: ToneSoul Cross-Agent Memory Consumer Contract
entries.purpose: keep Codex-style shells, Claude-style shells, and dashboard/operator surfaces aligned on how they read shared ToneSoul memory surfaces.

entries.title: ToneSoul — Doc Drift And Conflict Register
entries.purpose: compare the prior entrypoint cleanup recommendations against the current repo state and classify each as still valid, drifted, already fixed, or invalidated.

entries.title: ToneSoul — Doc Metric And Count Method
entries.purpose: define reproducible counting methods for doc families so later prose no longer casually says "46 contracts" when the actual directory shape means something different.

entries.title: ToneSoul Doc Surface Simplification Boundary Contract
entries.purpose: define which simplification moves are safe when reducing entrypoint complexity, and which moves would flatten authority or hide important depth.

entries.title: ToneSoul Eight-Layer Convergence Map
entries.purpose: reconcile ToneSoul's six operational governance layers with the newer retrieval and model-attachment layers without implying a hidden ninth layer.

entries.title: ToneSoul Encoding And Mojibake Hazard Register
entries.purpose: register encoding hazards, garbled headers, broken section labels, and mixed-layer readability issues in high-traffic documentation so cleanup can follow a clear severity order.

entries.title: ToneSoul — Entry Surface Reality Baseline
entries.purpose: repo-grounded baseline for current entry surfaces after correcting drift between the prior entrypoint pass and the live repo state.

entries.title: ToneSoul Evidence Ladder And Verifiability Contract
entries.purpose: define a small number of evidence levels that classify how strongly each ToneSoul claim is backed, what language is honest at each level, and what upgrade would move a claim higher

entries.title: ToneSoul Externalized Cognitive Architecture
entries.purpose: define ToneSoul as an externalized cognitive operating system where governance, memory, verification, and distillation stay outside model weights.

entries.title: ToneSoul Historical Spec And Legacy Surface Map
entries.purpose: separate current canonical surfaces from historical lineage, active companions, and legacy documents so later agents stop treating old specs as current architecture.

entries.title: ToneSoul Hot-Memory Decay And Compression Map
entries.purpose: explain how ToneSoul's current hot-memory ladder should decay, compress, or stay quarantined across successor-facing surfaces.

entries.title: ToneSoul Internal-State Observability Reality-Check Contract
entries.purpose: define how ToneSoul may externalize bounded functional pressure signals without turning observability into selfhood claims.

entries.title: ToneSoul Knowledge Layer Boundary Contract
entries.purpose: define where a future ToneSoul knowledge layer begins and ends, so retrieval does not collapse into R-memory, identity, or exploratory residue.

entries.title: ToneSoul L7 Retrieval Contract
entries.purpose: L7 `compiled retrieval + verifier surfaces`

entries.title: ToneSoul L8 Distillation Boundary Contract
entries.purpose: L8 `model attachment + distillation boundary`

entries.title: ToneSoul Launch Health Trend Readout Contract
entries.purpose: define how ToneSoul should separate present-tense launch posture from future trend or forecast surfaces.

entries.title: ToneSoul Law / Runtime Boundary Contract
entries.purpose: explicitly classify high-confusion terms from law/ and related deep prose into four categories so later agents stop mistaking theory for live implementation

entries.title: ToneSoul Low-Drift Anchor Source Precedence Contract
entries.purpose: define the one source-order story that later agents should use when reading low-drift anchor, canonical center, and observer-window surfaces.

entries.title: ToneSoul Mirror Rupture, Fail-Stop, And Low-Drift Anchor Contract
entries.purpose: define when ToneSoul should stop smooth mirroring, refuse L3 filler, and return current reasoning to a validated anchor center instead of continuing on drifted premises.

entries.title: ToneSoul Multi-Agent Semantic Field Contract
entries.purpose: multi-agent shared runtime memory, perspective lanes, semantic-field synthesis, and canonical commit safety
entries.status: architectural contract and rollout boundary as of 2026-03-26

entries.title: ToneSoul Observable-Shell Opacity Contract
entries.purpose: define what ToneSoul may claim as observable, partially observable, or opaque when describing runtime reasoning and auditability

entries.title: ToneSoul Plan Delta Contract
entries.purpose: define when later agents should keep a plan, append a bounded delta, fork a new phase, or stop and ask a human — instead of silently rewriting task.md or thrashing between plan revisions

entries.title: ToneSoul Prompt Discipline Skeleton
entries.purpose: translate high-signal prompt-architecture ideas into ToneSoul-native control-plane language so extraction, transfer, and summarization prompts preserve evidence, priority, and bounded continuity.
entries.status: active discipline skeleton and prompt design companion.

entries.title: ToneSoul Prompt Starter Cards
entries.purpose: provide short, ready-to-adapt starter cards derived from the Prompt Discipline Skeleton and Prompt Variants so later agents can begin with a bounded prompt shape instead of writing from zero.

entries.title: ToneSoul Prompt Surface Adoption Matrix
entries.purpose: classify meaningful prompt surfaces and prompt-adjacent helpers by adoption readiness, family, and risk so future prompt-discipline work follows topology instead of intuition.

entries.title: ToneSoul Prompt Surface Boundary Contract
entries.purpose: define when a prompt surface deserves starter-card adoption, when it only needs wording cleanup, and when it must remain specialized.

entries.title: ToneSoul Prompt Surface Topology Map
entries.purpose: group prompt surfaces into families so adoption can happen in bounded waves instead of one-off patches.

entries.title: ToneSoul Prompt Variants
entries.purpose: provide ToneSoul-native prompt variants built on top of the Prompt Discipline Skeleton so later agents can choose a task-shaped prompt pattern instead of improvising from scratch.
entries.status: active prompt-variant catalog and implementation companion.

entries.title: ToneSoul Receiver Interpretation Boundary Contract
entries.purpose: define what a receiving agent is and is not allowed to do after reading continuity surfaces — focusing on agent behavior rather than surface classification

entries.title: ToneSoul — Render Layer And Encoding Boundary Contract
entries.purpose: explicitly separate real file corruption from terminal rendering noise, structural readability hazards, and metadata drift, so later agents stop calling every `??` display artifact "mojibake".

entries.title: ToneSoul Runtime Compaction And Gamification Contract
entries.purpose: runtime compaction memory, dashboard/world gamification, legacy trace repair, and security sidecar work
entries.status: architectural contract and prioritization boundary as of 2026-03-26

entries.title: ToneSoul Shared R-Memory Operations Contract
entries.purpose: multi-agent shared runtime operations across diagnose, packet, claims, perspectives, checkpoints, compactions, subject snapshots, and serialized canonical commit

entries.title: ToneSoul Subject Refresh Boundary Contract
entries.purpose: classify which source surfaces may refresh which subject_snapshot field families, and under what minimum evidence standard

entries.title: ToneSoul Subject Snapshot Field Lanes
entries.purpose: classify subject_snapshot fields into four lanes by their refresh tolerance so the boundary contract matrix is readable at a glance
